sub_pixel_system.notes: No sub-pixel system documentation available for this beat'em up title

special_mechanics.name: Temporary Invincibility Mode
special_mechanics.description: Character enters an 'angry' mode where they become temporarily invincible and more powerful after taking a certain amount of damage

special_mechanics.name: Grab and Throw System
special_mechanics.description: Characters can grab enemies and perform special attacks. Japanese version allows groin kicks with distinctive sound effect; Western version uses standard hit animation

special_mechanics.name: Flying Kick Attack
special_mechanics.description: Jack Flak's specialty attack, a projectile-style kick move

special_mechanics.name: Professional Wrestling Moves
special_mechanics.description: Oozie Nelson uses powerful professional wrestling-based moves including back drops as specialty attacks

special_mechanics.name: Character Knockback
special_mechanics.description: Regional difference noted: Japanese version allows getting hit while grabbing an enemy; US version removed this mechanic for balance

tier_notes: Tier 3 (empirical/qualitative) - No quantitative physics parameters documented in available sources. Game is a beat'em up rather than platformer, limiting traditional movement physics data. Critical reception noted deficiencies in collision detection and physics implementation. All documented information derives from design differences between regional versions and gameplay descriptions rather than technical specifications.
sources_used: tcrf - The Cutting Room Floor (Rival Turf! article)
